text.title: Spring Async Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for executor naming, pool sizing, context propagation, return types, and graceful shutdown.

rule.statement: Always name the executor in @Async: @Async("emailExecutor"), never plain @Async.
rule.rationale: The default executor changes between Spring Boot versions (SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor in Boot 3.2+ with virtual threads enabled); unnamed @Async silently picks up the wrong pool.

rule.statement: Define separate named ThreadPoolTaskExecutors for IO-bound pools with different SLAs (e.g., emailExecutor, taskExecutor).
rule.rationale: A single shared pool lets email storms starve general tasks and vice versa; separation allows independent sizing and monitoring.

rule.statement: Set RejectedExecutionHandler to CallerRunsPolicy for backpressure.
rule.rationale: The default AbortPolicy throws RejectedExecutionException under load; CallerRunsPolicy slows the caller thread instead of dropping work.

rule.statement: Size pool by cores * (1 + wait_ratio / compute_ratio) for IO-bound tasks; profile, do not guess.
rule.rationale: Under-sizing blocks callers; over-sizing wastes threads and increases context-switching overhead. IO-bound tasks spend most time waiting, so higher multipliers are appropriate.

rule.statement: Set setWaitForTasksToCompleteOnShutdown(true) and setAwaitTerminationSeconds(30) on every executor.
rule.rationale: Without graceful shutdown, in-flight async tasks are aborted on JVM shutdown — silent data loss for email or audit jobs.

rule.statement: Return CompletableFuture<T> from every @Async method; never return void or Future<T>.
rule.rationale: void @Async methods swallow exceptions unless AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler is configured; CompletableFuture makes exceptions observable and supports composition.

rule.statement: Register an AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler when any void @Async method exists in the codebase.
rule.rationale: Without a handler, exceptions from void @Async methods are logged at WARN by Spring at most, and lost in many configurations.

rule.statement: Wrap the executor with a TaskDecorator that propagates MDC, SecurityContext, and tracing baggage.
rule.rationale: @Async runs on a different thread; MDC request-id, SecurityContext user, and OpenTelemetry span context do not propagate automatically without a decorator.

rule.statement: Never call an @Async method from within the same bean instance; inject a separate bean or use AopContext.currentProxy().
rule.rationale: Spring AOP proxies only intercept external calls; a same-bean call bypasses the proxy and runs synchronously with no error or warning.

text.reference: ThreadPoolTaskExecutor config with CallerRunsPolicy, graceful shutdown, TaskDecorator
